# Label

A form field row — label, input, and help text. The for attribute has been the accessibility API this whole time.

```html
<div class="grid w-full max-w-xs gap-1.5">
  <label for="lbl-email" class="text-sm leading-none font-medium">Email</label>
  <input
    id="lbl-email"
    type="email"
    placeholder="you@example.com"
    class="h-9 w-full rounded-lg border border-input bg-background px-3 text-sm transition-colors placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-ring"
  />
  <p class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">We'll never share it with anyone.</p>
</div>
```

## How it works

- `for` + `id` links label to input: clicking focuses, and screen readers announce the label
  with the field.
- `grid gap-1.5` handles the vertical rhythm; the help text is just a muted `<p>`.
- Add `aria-describedby` pointing at the help text if it carries information the user must hear.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

None. This component uses only Baseline CSS/HTML — it works the same in every current browser.
